Home / Alternatives to Harvest / Harvest vs Kimai

Harvest vs Kimai

A side-by-side look at Harvest (the paid SaaS) and Kimai (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Harvest Kimai
Tagline Time tracking with invoicing built in. Self-hosted time tracker with invoicing and reports.
License Proprietary SaaS AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free for 1 user; Pro $10.80/user/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available iOS, Android
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

The closest self-hosted match for Harvest's timesheet-plus-invoicing workflow.

Kimai strengths

  • Teams, projects, customers and invoices in one tool.
  • REST API plus mobile clients.
  • Easy PHP/MySQL deployment on any VPS.

Kimai weaknesses

  • UI is functional rather than polished.
  • Some reports need the paid plugins or plugin-store extensions.
  • Smaller ecosystem of integrations than Toggl.

What's the catch with Harvest?

  • Project structure is rigid for complex agencies.
  • Integrations feel dated compared to newer entrants.
  • Invoicing is basic — many teams pair it with a second tool.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Harvest: see Harvest alternatives, or learn more about Kimai on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
